title: [lora_l2.c:560-631] lichen_lora_l2_stop: return 0 on idempotent paths but doc says 'always returns 0'
description:
Contract violation: postcondition/documentation

**Issue:** The docstring says 'Always returns 0' but the function can return -ECANCELED on line 627 when thread abort occurs.

**Header doc (lora_l2.h:142):**
```
 * @return Always returns 0 (stop is idempotent and cannot fail)
```

**Implementation:**
```c
// line 626-627
lora_transition(LORA_ABORTED);
ret = -ECANCELED;
...
return ret;  // Can be -ECANCELED
```

**Impact:** Callers trusting the doc that stop() cannot fail will not check return value and miss detecting the ABORTED state requiring reinit.

**Fix:** Update header documentation to reflect that -ECANCELED is returned when forced abort occurs.
close_reason: Fixed: updated header doc to document -ECANCELED return on forced abort
